# dist/ — go4-fashion skill bundle (temporary delivery channel)

This directory exists because **Anthropic's Pro plan does not support automatic plugin distribution**. It packages the same content as the Claude Code plugin into a single uploadable Cowork skill (`go4-fashion.zip`) that managers on Pro plans can install manually.

This is a **workaround**, not the long-term plan. See "When this goes away" below.

## What this is

| File / dir | Purpose |
|---|---|
| `go4-fashion/` | Source of the skill bundle. Contains `SKILL.md` (entry point), `knowledge/` (read once per session), `workflows/` (read on demand). Edit these files to change skill behavior. |
| `go4-fashion.zip` | Prebuilt artifact regenerated by `build.sh`. Committed to the repo so customers can download directly via GitHub raw URL. |
| `build.sh` | Regenerates `go4-fashion.zip` from `go4-fashion/`. Run after every change. |
| `INSTALL.md` | End-user (manager) instructions in Polish. Sent to customers along with the ZIP. |
| `README.md` | This file. Maintainer documentation. |

## Why this exists — the decision

The plugin (`/skills`, `/agents`, `.claude-plugin/plugin.json` at the repo root) is the **long-term target** for shipping go4.fashion intelligence to Claude. Plugins support:

- automatic installation from a GitHub-backed marketplace,
- automatic updates on every PR merge,
- bundled MCP server config + skills + agents in one artifact.

**Three delivery channels exist for plugins. Two require Team or Enterprise plans:**

| Channel | Plan required | Auto-update | UX |
|---|---|---|---|
| Claude Code `/plugin install` | Any | Yes (`/plugin update`) | Developer tool — Michał uses this, customers do not |
| Org-managed private marketplace (Cowork) | **Team or Enterprise** | Yes (auto on PR merge) | Best — customer admin sets it up once, plugin auto-installs and auto-updates for all members |
| Public Anthropic plugin directory (Cowork) | Pro and up | Yes (Anthropic-managed) | Best — one-click install from Browse plugins, no admin required |

**Studio B3 — our first customer — is on Pro plan, not Team.** Team plan has a minimum of 5 seats (~$125–150/month), which is overkill for a 1–2 person fashion brand and would burn $1000+/year for unused seats just to enable plugin distribution. Public directory submission requires Anthropic review with no documented timeline and turns the plugin into a publicly visible product, which is not where we want to be in early phase.

**Conclusion:** until either (a) B3 grows to a size where Team plan makes sense, or (b) the plugin is accepted into the public Anthropic directory, we need a fourth delivery channel that works on Pro. That channel is the manual skill upload — `Customize → Skills → Upload a skill` — which accepts a single ZIP. This directory builds that ZIP from the same source as the plugin.

## Why one mega-skill instead of 12 separate skills

The skill bundle uploads as **one** skill, not twelve. The plugin format has 12 separate skills (`skills/go4-domain`, `skills/billing`, `skills/import-order`, etc.), each with its own `SKILL.md` and frontmatter. Cowork's upload UI accepts one ZIP per skill, so distributing 12 skills would mean 12 uploads — unacceptable UX for a non-technical manager.

The bundle structure inside `go4-fashion/`:

```
go4-fashion/
├── SKILL.md                    # entry point: short router (~100 lines)
│                                 lists workflows + tells Claude to read knowledge/ once
├── knowledge/
│   ├── domain-model.md         # entities, relationships, universal rules
│   └── rag-protocol.md         # how to use knowledge_search / knowledge_capture
└── workflows/
    ├── import-order.md
    ├── billing.md
    ├── new-expense.md
    ├── monthly-report.md
    ├── sync-products.md
    ├── ship.md
    ├── manage-bom.md
    ├── import-collection.md
    ├── label-composition.md
    └── order-materials.md
```

`SKILL.md` is the entry point that Claude loads when the skill is triggered. It is intentionally short — it tells Claude what go4.fashion is, points at `knowledge/` files for context, and provides a router table mapping manager intent to the matching `workflows/*.md` file. Claude reads only the workflow file relevant to the current task — full content is not loaded into context unnecessarily. This follows Anthropic's recommended pattern for skills: short SKILL.md + supporting files loaded on demand.

## How updates work today

1. Source of truth: `skills/<name>/SKILL.md` (plugin) **and** `dist/go4-fashion/workflows/<name>.md` (skill bundle). Both must change together.
2. After editing, run `./dist/build.sh` to rebuild `dist/go4-fashion.zip`.
3. Commit and push everything together.
4. Send the customer this URL to download the latest ZIP:
   ```
   https://github.com/michaldyrda/go4-plugin/raw/main/dist/go4-fashion.zip
   ```
5. Customer manager: `Customize → Skills → delete go4-fashion → upload new ZIP`. ~30 seconds.

This is manual and friction-heavy on purpose — we accept it as the cost of being on Pro plan. It is not how we want this to work forever.

## How customers get to the bundle

Two artifacts are sent to a new customer once:

1. **`INSTALL.md`** — Polish setup instructions (connector + skill upload + first test).
2. **`go4-fashion.zip`** — the bundle, downloaded from `https://github.com/michaldyrda/go4-plugin/raw/main/dist/go4-fashion.zip`.

The customer also needs to add the go4 MCP server as a custom connector once (URL: `https://web-production-bdb8f.up.railway.app/mcp`, OAuth login). The skill provides the instructions and reasoning; the MCP connector provides the actual tools. **Both must be present** for the system to work — the skill alone is just text, the MCP connector alone is just tools without context.

## Relationship to the plugin (`/skills`, `/agents`)

The plugin and the skill bundle ship the **same content** through different formats. Maintenance rule (also recorded in the repo's CLAUDE.md):

> Every change to skill content must be reflected in **both** `skills/` (plugin) **and** `dist/go4-fashion/` (skill bundle) before commit. Then run `./dist/build.sh` to refresh the ZIP.

Things that exist only in the plugin (not in the bundle):
- **Agents** (`agents/billing-agent.md`, etc.) — Cowork does not support agents at all. They are a Claude Code feature and would be silently ignored if included.
- **MCP server config** (`.claude-plugin/plugin.json` `mcpServers` block) — bundled inside plugins, but skills cannot bundle MCP. Customers add the connector separately via Settings → Connectors.
- **Slash commands** (`/go4-fashion:billing` etc.) — plugin skills can register commands, skill-bundle skills cannot. The bundle relies on natural language ("wystaw fakturę dla X").

Everything else — domain knowledge, RAG protocol, workflow steps, two-stage previews, tool usage patterns — is identical between the two.

## When this goes away

This `dist/` directory should be deleted when **either** of these is true:

1. **Studio B3 (and any other Pro-tier customer) upgrades to Team plan.** At that point we set up an org-managed private marketplace pointing at this repo, and the plugin format takes over with auto-updates. No more manual ZIP delivery.
2. **Anthropic accepts the plugin into the public Cowork directory.** Customers — including those on Pro — install it via `Browse plugins` with one click. Updates handled by Anthropic.

Until one of those happens, this directory stays. Do not delete `dist/` "to clean up the repo" — it is the only delivery channel that works for Pro-plan customers right now, and it ships real software to a real customer.

## Quick reference

```bash
# After editing skill content (in both skills/ and dist/go4-fashion/):
./dist/build.sh

# Customer download URL (always points at the latest committed ZIP):
https://github.com/michaldyrda/go4-plugin/raw/main/dist/go4-fashion.zip

# Customer install instructions:
https://github.com/michaldyrda/go4-plugin/blob/main/dist/INSTALL.md
```
